title: API Resources, Object Identity, Schemas, Desired State, and Status · Kubernetes Retrieval
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs api resources, object identity, schemas, desired state, and status from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the versioned API object contract under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for versioned API object contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve API group, version, kind, plural resource, scope, endpoint, and served or stored version for a concrete object.
Separate desired spec, controller-owned status, metadata generation, observedGeneration, and status conditions before judging convergence.
Use namespace, name, UID, resourceVersion, generation, timestamps, and managed fields to distinguish identity, concurrency, and history.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Api Stability Deprecation
steps.instruction:
review case — A review manifest is valid YAML but uses a removed API, contains an unknown field, and reports observedGeneration 2 behind generation 3. Establish discovery, schema, identity, spec, status, conditions, and compatibility evidence. Competency probe: Evaluate alpha, beta, stable, feature-gate, deprecation, removal, conversion, and version-skew evidence before selecting an API.

Inspect the K8s Api Stability Deprecation trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review K8s Api Stability Deprecation, keep K8s Api Stability Deprecation connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies K8s Api Stability Deprecation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.
